text: To be honest, I am Starbucks' biggest hater. BUT, we had to check out the Starbucks Reserve, and I am now extremely happy to report that I have a new item added to my list of top favorite eats EVER. The Matcha Margarita was one of the three drinks that came on the Seasonal Cocktail flight from the Arriviamo Bar. It is one of the best cocktails I have ever had. This margarita was a perfect balance of a punch of tequila contrasted with the mellowness of matcha. With a slight, lime flavor, this drink comes together so beautifully in the way it combines cocktail and coffee. I really have no notes for improvement. What really makes it stand out is the matcha cold foam (like how genius is that). Double Matcha Margarita El Tesoro Blanco Tequila, matcha, lime juice, agave nectar, matcha cold foam. Highly highly recommend all of the drinks in this trio. The Ube Espresso Martini was also insane - a classic espresso martini topped with ube cold foam. Because honestly, we all want cold foam on our espresso martinis. The classic espresso martini trio was solid, with my favorites being the lemon martini and orange martini. Starbucks Reserve... you did something with these drinks that I'll be thinking about for a while!

My visit to the Starbucks Reserve Roastery Chicago was a great experience overall. The line to get in moved fairly quickly, which was a nice surprise considering how popular it is. That being said, because it's such a busy and popular spot, there was a fair amount of waiting throughout the experience. The roastery has four floors, and each one offers something different. The first floor is mainly for merchandise and was the quickest to get through. The second floor is the specialty cafe where you can order drinks and food. The third floor features the experimental coffee bar, which is fun if you're interested in trying more unique coffee options. The fourth floor serves alcoholic beverages, and that's where we had the Ube Espresso Martini, easily a 10/10. All of the employees we interacted with were pleasant and helpful. One thing to keep in mind: if something is sold out on one floor, they might still have it available on another floor, so it's worth checking. Overall, it was a really cool experience and definitely worth visiting if you're in the area.

While visiting Chicago, I was brought here by a local. Since I came during a winter storm, there was no line! We were greeted warmly by staff at the front. The first level mainly consists of souvenirs and to go orders. They also had the whiskey infused coffee! I went up the other floors and of course, I loved the cocktail floor! This floor had tiramisu espresso martini, which was so amazing! I sipped this as I gazed at the art that spanned from the first floor all the way to top! I also loved looking at the various types of tea that decorated the walls. They definitely have delicious looking food, better than any Starbucks that I've visited. Although a little busy, we were able to get a table so that we can enjoy our drinks and people watch. This definitely is a must visit when you're in Chicago.

I am not a Starbucks fanatic, but this was interesting to see while in town! My tiramisu latte was great & with a reusable cup. It came to $12. The second floor has savory food, the third floor had sweet food, and the 4th floor was the bar. We saw lots of pretty drinks coming from the bar but it was over a 30 minute wait so we did not get anything there. Service was adequate. Lots of seating inside and funky displays to look at.

First time to a Starbucks Reserve. Glad they had someone at the door to explain the layout. First floor is food and coffee to go. Third floor had a long line for seating so I took the escalator up to a quiet bar. Their mini martini flights are very expensive ($30-35) and I was disappointed that they don't allow for any substitutions - like a classic instead of a flavored. Seems like a heavy use of flavored sugar syrups to deliver their flavor of their trendy drinks - they look fancy but I'm not looking for a sugar bomb.

Starbucks roastery shops are always interesting to visit if you go to one of the cities that has one. Each floor is dedicated to a specific purpose, you could be food centered, coffee centered, or alcohol centered. Each location has their own bean blend too! This time, because it was very busy, I just got a coffee to quickly drink. It was delectable! I've tried their alcohol flights at another location but I would definitely be interested in coming back to try the pour over coffee flight with the Chi specific blend! PS, the highlight of the visit was the escalator! I've never seen a rotating spiral escalator before!!

The Starbucks Reserve in Chicago is four levels of awesomeness. Such a cool experience from top to bottom -- incredible coffee, delicious pastries, coffee-infused martinis, pizza, and great sandwiches. There's something to try on every floor, and the atmosphere makes it more than just a coffee stop. Definitely a must-visit when in Chicago!

I really had to visit the Chicago Starbucks Reserve Roastery, smack-dab in the middle of The Magnificent Mile of shopping. It's the largest Starbucks location in the world! There's four floors and a rooftop terrace. There's escalators, elevators, and stairs going up. Note that the escalators don't go all way back down to the ground level(!). It's all shiny and woody, with some stylish mid-room fireplaces (Feb 2026), lots of tables, and some barstools. The first three floors are about coffee, and the lines can be quite long, especially on the first two floors, with the first floor dedicated to takeout and some merch. Midday on a Sunday, the lines were so long, maybe 100 people waiting on the second floor, that I nearly postponed to another time. But the 3rd floor had only a dozen or so people waiting, and the friendly hardworking staff was quite efficient. They had Americano and espresso, and an array of baked goods and sandwiches, too. I came for espresso, and they had three Reserve roasts available at the time. There's plenty of seating around the large room. I was lucky to find an open barstool at the counter! When my buzzer went off after 5 or so minutes, they brought me luxurious ceramic cups and glasses of water on a handsome pewter-grey-color tray. The espressos were piping hot. Their own farm's Costa Rica Hacienda Alsacia Decaf espresso was a pleasant medium-to-dark roast, a little sour with a subtly fruity flavor, and a light aroma. The 1971 Dark, with Colombian, Sumatran, and Brazilian beans in homage to their founding blend, had a complex, lightly bitter, dusty flavor, with a pleasantly sour aroma. The strong Chicago Roastery Microblend leaned towards sweet fruity flavor, with intoxicating cocoa aroma. I sipped them slowly, in rotation, breaking between them with ice-cold water, staring unfocussed, probably sighing. I lingered over my mid-day espressos. Today's experience was exactly what i hoped for. I'll surely be back to try the 4th-floor espresso cocktails and the rooftop terrace.

I had the best time at the Starbucks reserve Roastery in Chicago. I was visiting Chicago from out of town and hotel was a few blocks away from here and I had to stop in and check it out. I walked in and instantly saw huge people everywhere waiting in line. I had just eaten, so luckily I wasn't tempted by any of the food, but I check out the merch that they had here and also get a drink. I was a little overwhelmed by all the different levels and they were different. I can tell the difference between the first and third floor with the offerings were. But I seem like they were there for drinks. On the second floor is where they had food items. On the fifth floor is where they had the bar. The decor inside and on every floor was unique and you could tell people are having a great time. I eventually found that one of the fourth out are shorted line and I ordered my mocha coffee. I was also able to buy the mug I wanted to get.

I'm not a Starbucks fan but I'm impressed with Starbucks Reserve Roastery in Chicago. Located on 4 Floors, this cafe is impressive. From standing outside, you can't miss the 56 Foot copper cask which is a multi story copper cylinder where coffee beans rest after roasting. The 1st floor is mostly retail items and the main roastery, 2nd floor is the bakery serving up sandwiches, breads, pastries, 3rd floor is the experimental bar, 4th floor is the coffee cocktail area. It was winter time, so we tried the espresso flight. For $27, it's a waste. Basically a trio of espresso with different flavored creams. But 3 is like the volume of 1 and charging you premium for a flight. Regardless, it's one of the nice cafes to visit in Chicago and a must visit.

We visited the Reserve multiple times during our Chicago trip since we are massive Starbucks fans. While we enjoyed the coffee roasting and tasting experience, it wasn't absolutely necessary. You can still have a great time just visiting, shopping for merch, and ordering a specialty drink and pastry. We had to do it since we're hardcore fans and have even been to the coffee farm in Costa Rica. But once was definitely enough for us. I really enjoyed the pistachio croissant and the hot chocolate flight, even though I'm not a big hot chocolate person. While I begrudgingly bought the expensive burgundy Chicago sweatshirt at the time, I'm really glad I did. It's so soft, and I find myself reaching for it often. I recommend going with a friend so you can divide and conquer: one of you tracks down a nice place to sit while the other gets in line to order drinks and food.

300th review and we got a winner here, baby!! So I am not a Starbucks fanatic - it's not my favorite coffee and it has gotten too expensive - but I am a Starbucks RESERVE fanatic! There are only 5 of these high concept coffee experiences in the world. Fortunately, one of these five is nearby in Chicago. Opened in 2019 and located in a 35,000 square foot building on the Magnificent Mile, you truly cannot miss seeing this massive, gorgeous building while touring the city. On this most recent visit in late December of 2025, we had nearly no line at 9am but it got veryyyyy busy just 30 minutes later. After thoroughly researching the menu and scoping out what they had out on trays, we both got a prosciutto herb croissant ($9 each), a masala chai latte ($7.50), and a cold brew with sweet cream cold foam ($7). My total after tax and tip was $41.19. YIKES! We didn't get a dessert this time around, but on our first visit here in December 2024, we split two magnificent desserts and gained about 5 pounds doing so. But my god, the croissant was heavenly. My Bf was obsessed with the spices in his masala chai tea and my coffee, while not anything out-of-the-box, was made well. I do wish they had sugar free cold foam here. You'll have to fight hundreds of other tourists to get a seat somewhere so don't be surprised if you chow down on $40 worth of food standing over a trash can... but you gotta for the vibesssssss.

Fave spot Morning Noon Night. Anytime so worth and the hype!!! Four levels. Level one, the regular inside of the Starbucks order your drink and that's it. Yes they do have Starbucks reserve merch that is exclusive only for that store. Second floor, the fun and reserve drinks. And food, of course the food and the drink drinks. Third floor more of the reserve drinks and food is there too. Fourth floor the 21+ floor, the one that serves alcohol and they have food. Bring your photo ID because they do check your age. As well, your reserve points will not be added to the card of yours. Fifth floor, the balcony yes you can order your drink and you will get it up there, but mostly is the experience. Please reserve this. Customer service is fantastic there everyone is super nice chill easy-going. My ultimate favorite is the pumpkin spice espresso martini, old fashion, and the eggnog martini. Food wise their pizza is phenomenal, and the peppermint cookie is really good.

First time at Starbucks reserve and once I opened the doors you get the smell of coffee immediately and greeted by a friendly staff member. The first thing I notice was the decor and I started taking pictures and video. We took the escalator to the 2nd floor where we ordered coffee, pastries and savory croissants. The tiramisu hot latte was out of this world delicious! The caprese on herb croissant is a must try. After we finished our coffee we took the escalator to the 4th floor where we sat at the bar and stayed there for about 2 hours. Wished every Starbucks served The cold brew spice rum. It's sooooo good. Definitely one of those drinks I'll never forget.

Crowded, hard to find seats, line for ordering, line for the bathroom, we ordered the tiramisu latte which was not good and nothing special compared regular Starbucks coffee. The inside of the building was cool if you've never been to a reserve roastery. Maybe I would have enjoyed it more if I signed up for one of their coffee roasting or cocktail making experiences but they were sold out and the cocktail flight itself was really small from the customers ordering it and it all seemed overpriced.

A solid way to start our week! Our flight left in the afternoon and check out was early, so we had some time to kill. The space in itself is really beautiful and designed really uniquely. Each floor has something different, it's a true coffee lovers experience! We started with breakfast at the second floor cafe. We got breakfast sandwiches and cold brew. I enjoyed both, their seasonal flavors were really delicious. We made our way to the third floor which is the cocktails. We ordered the seasonal espresso martini flight (still thinking about it) and an old fashioned. I really loved my mini martinis, the peppermint was my favorite. We made our way downstairs where there is tons of merch to look at. It's a must see when you're in Chi!

While staying in Chicago, one of the concierge at my hotel had recommended that if I had never been at a Starbucks reserve roaster that I had to make the trip and check it out. Well I assumed it would be bigger. I did not expect it to be as big as this place actually was. Looking like some sort of multi-floor Glass lounge. It was a pretty impressive building to see. When I walked in there were people there to help greet me and answer any questions I had. I took a look around to check out some of their merch at Starbucks. They had a pretty cool selection of cups to choose from and some pretty cool styles. They also have some select coffee beans on sale that you can only get at through the reserve. Roastery. However, I thought they were a little expensive. They had a bourbon barrel one that was recommended at about $42 a pound that happened to also just be a medium roast. They also have the ability to come in and speak with roasters around noon each day. And there is also a tasting thing. You can book tickets for upstairs that if you have the free time I think might be worth doing and would recommend reserving in advance.

This is not your typical Starbucks. It's a world class coffee experience. Right in the middle of the Magnificent Mile, this place is massive. Multiple floors, a rooftop terrace, escalators, elevators, the whole thing. Each level has its own vibe and purpose, which honestly makes it fun to explore instead of just grabbing a drink and leaving. The design is gorgeous. Lots of warm wood, copper details, and open space. Each floor offers something different. The main level has your classic drinks and pastries. The upper floors get more creative with specialty brews, coffee flights, cocktails, and even barrel-aged coffee. You could easily spend an hour just walking around and deciding what to try. It does get busy. Lines can be long, especially on the lower floors, but if you go up a level it's usually more manageable.

Recently visited the Starbucks Reserve Roastery in Chicago and had a good overall experience. We went on a late Friday night around 9pm. With five floors, there should be something for every coffee fan, whether it is the Reserve Coffee Bar on the first floor, the Reserve Cafe on the second, the Experiential Coffee Bar on the third, or the Cocktail Bar on the fourth. I was excited to try the classic espresso martini, and since there was still an hour before closing, we headed upstairs. After waiting at the bar for a few minutes, a bartender informed us that they stop making drinks an hour before closing. We also learned that the rooftop terrace on the fifth floor was closed, which was another disappointment.

Visiting Starbucks Reserve Roastery on The Magnificent Mile was absolutely dreamy...five floors of coffee heaven right in one of Chicago's most iconic, touristy spots. The hype is 100% real. As a lifelong Starbucks fan, stepping inside this Roastery felt like entering a magical coffee universe. The design, the energy, the aroma of freshly roasted coffee, everything was incredible. I treated myself to the Peppermint Espresso Martini, one of their holiday special, and it was perfection. They also offer a tasting flight, but I skipped it since I was exploring solo. Still, just enjoying this city, the Starbucks, and soaking it all in made me so happy, it's a full-on experience, not just a coffee stop.

Starbucks Reserve Roastery Chicago is a landmark Starbucks location that contains a full bar, restaurant, grab and go gift shop, and of course Starbucks drinks. It's a tourist destination that I didn't mind getting trapped in. I spent my time on the 3rd floor, where a full, epic bar was the center piece and beckoned me to grab a seat. I grabbed an open seat at the bar, and the bartenders gave me a menu. Unlike most Starbucks, I could order food and alcohol-based coffee drinks. The best part of sitting at the bar was watching the bartenders expertly craft each drink. I ordered the Cold Brew Spiced Rum. This beautiful drink was served cold with a single clear ice cube. The drink was slightly sweet and spiced and it was paired perfectly with an Olive Oil cake.

I've been here multiple times for their espresso martini flights! They have a fantastic espresso martini that is not overpoweringly coffee flavored or sweet. It's this perfect balance. The flights are fun because you get to try some unique flavors. They have a fall and holiday one that features their classic martini with seasonal flavors. This is a great place to take anyone visiting because the reserve is the largest Starbucks in the world and each floor does provide something unique. There's a small outdoor rooftop for the nicer days. Tip: it's better to come here during the weekday vs weekend because the weekends have huge lines and it's harder to get to the bar!

Visually stunning. The massive copper machinery, the soaring ceilings, and the constant sound of beans roasting make it feel grand and energetic. It's a fantastic spot for people-watching or a unique date by their fireplace! The coffee selection is incredible, featuring rare selected small-lot Reserve beans and unique preparation methods. Coffee and pastry items from around the world. They also have a full bar and excellent, high-quality artisanal food. You watch the entire process, from green bean to cup. The staff are highly knowledgeable and passionate. I tried their recommended Prosciutto Parmigiano-Regianno on herb Cornetto it was scrumptious, with Tiramisu Latte. Everything is significantly more expensive than a regular Starbucks. It can get extremely busy and noisy, making it difficult to find a seat or a quiet moment.

LOVELY TIME HERE. I'm not the biggest coffee drinker, actually I used to drink a cup a day but quit a few months ago! However, I knew that I NEEDED to try the espresso martini flight at the Starbucks Reserve Roastery. It was delightful to say the least, my favorite out of the three flavors (orange cacao, mole spice, and cherry limone) was the orange cacao! I also couldn't resist and ordered the matcha margarita...it was so delicious, I honestly would be satisfied if I just got that! For snacks, I got the tiramisu (DELISH) and pear almond tart (it was okay). If you are planning on going here, please know that it gets super busy and seating is limited. If you are wanting to try out their drinks, it is on the fourth floor! Anything below the fourth floor do not sell alcoholic drinks.

Surprisingly, there was no line here at 2 PM. I ordered directly from the barista and waited no more than eight minutes. The pistachio cookie was delicious. Assaulted caramel latte was delicious. It was a little sweet, but I do like my Starbucks drinks sweet.

Living the experience of visiting one of the few Starbucks Reserve locations in the world was truly incredible. Despite the large crowds, the service was fast, the atmosphere was warm, and that coffee-infused air filled with rich aromas was simply delicious. I would absolutely go back; this time with more calm, taking my time to enjoy every detail on each of the five floors. The only downside was that the rooftop terrace was closed due to the winter season having already begun.

I came here many times and have liked the window-side seats. I think you mostly pay for the view and location at this roastery. I've tried: ICED CARAMEL BRULEE EGGNOG LATTE (newest drink in fall/winter 2025): tooooooooo sweeeeeeeeet. the color-changing tea experience: tea was meh. matcha float: was alright, slightly too sweet. but for $15 honestly... taro sakura drink: this was the most decent seasonal drink I've had. Regular menu: bianca mocha latte: very good, smooth and nutty. whisky coffee flight: I liked the lemon one best. Ambiance and service: It can get very crowdy during weekends. Baristas were mostly nice. Security downstairs can be very pushy when it's around the closing time.

Was in town and had to stop by the Starbucks Reserve even if the temperatures outside were not my favorite. We walked in and were greeted immediately and offered a sample coffee. They had a retail area with some items for purchase. We took the escalator up to the 2nd floor where they had more food and drink options. The kids all picked a snack, but went to the 3rd floor to get some of the specialty drinks. The adults then ventured up to the 4th floor for the cocktail options. We found seating on the 3rd floor and all hung out for a bit enjoying the different items. The tiramisu martini and the matcha margarita were my favorites!! Such a great hang out for a group to enjoy and get out of the cold weather!

The Starbucks Reserve Roastery in Chicago is truly out of this world! Spread across five stunning floors. Service: The staff were warm, friendly, and knowledgeable. They took their time explaining the different specialty drinks and pastries unique to this location. Food: I tried the Chocolate Hazelnut Cornetto paired with a Cold Brew Malt topped with caramel drizzle both were incredible! Ambience: The atmosphere is simply amazing. We sat on the third floor, enjoyed the view, and just relaxed all afternoon.

This place is amazing! Love these reserve locations. They have unique items, drinks, and decor at each location. At this specific location, they have 5 levels including the rooftop terrace when beautiful weather permits. The 4th floor is where you can get cocktail service. Espresso martini was delicious. I also opted for a flight of coffee. Cold brew was smooth and creamy. Of course, affogato is a must for those ice cream and shots lovers.

Came at night for a couple espresso martinis with my friends and they were SO GOOD. We were really excited to try the fall flavors, and we ended up ordering a couple of the flights. We got the Pumpkin Spice Cocktail flight and wow they were so good! My favorite was definitely the Pumpkin Spice Espresso Martini and then the Pumpkin Spice Whiskey Barrel. I ended up going back to order a full size Pumpkin Spice Espresso Martini for myself after I finished it. We came at like 8pm on a Saturday and were able to get our drinks quickly and find somewhere to sit.

This truly is such an awesome, unique Starbucks experience!! On level 4, there's a cocktail bar - which is what we primarily visited when we came. There was quite the long line to order, but it moves relatively quickly and we were able to get our food and drink items and find seating along the bar. I went for the Espresso Martini Flight and one of their seasonal Matcha Swirl Butterfly cakes - we also got an Ube Espresso Martini and some of the Margherita Flatbread. Everything we had was great!! My Espresso Martini Flight had great subtle notes of different flavors - with one being a bit citrusy and orange-y, while another was a bit spicier with some mole. On the ground floor is a large gift shop area, where I bought a few souvenir items.

I first visited the Roastery during COVID, but most of the floors were closed, so we had to come back for a second visit. It was busy on every floor, so we ended up on the top level, where they serve alcoholic beverages and subtly deter away families with little ones. I saw the seasonal Pumpkin Spice Espresso Martini and had to try something new. It was delicious and surprisingly strong which made it definitely worth the price.

This place is super touristy and really crowded during the weekend. They do have unique menu items and experiences, which draws tourists. There's a bar for coffee flight tasting, there's coffee cocktails you can order, and you can buy beans that are exclusive to either the specific store or Starbucks Reserve. One of the limited edition coffee beans is the one aged on Knob Creek whiskey barrels, that go for $40 for a regular size bag. Overall, it is a unique experience. Try to go during weekday morning to beat the crowd.

As someone who doesn't care about coffee, I actually think this Starbucks is worth a stop if you're visiting Chicago. There are 4 levels plus a small rooftop terrace. The bottom floor is merch and to-go drinks. Floor 2 is the bakery/cafe. Floor 3 is an experiential coffee bar where you can try brews only available there. Floor 4 is the full bar that sells coffee cocktails. We decided to indulge there. If you see a seat open at the bar you are able to order there instead of standing in line, but you can't order at the bar unless you have a seat. While the Roastery Old Fashioned and the Matcha Margarita sounded enticing, I went with the Cold Brew Spiced Rum. I loved the combinations of flavors. My bf ordered the Whiskey Cloud which is more coffee-forward and comes with a fun do-it-yourself presentation.

This was our first time visiting a Starbucks Reserve Roastery. The space itself is impressive -- modern, energetic, and thoughtfully designed. The curved escalator that takes you to the second floor is a cool architectural touch. Each floor offers something a little different. We visited in the winter, so the rooftop was closed, but we spent time at the cocktail bar instead. We tried the Tiramisu Martini and the Old Fashioned, and both were surprisingly excellent -- true craft cocktail quality. We also ordered the olive oil cake and a donut, both solid choices. We also purchased a couple bags of fresh beans that were scooped and sealed right in front of us.

If you like Starbucks, you'd be in heaven in this place! This is a 4 story Starbucks on Michigan Avenue. It's right next door to our hotel so we've been here every day on our visit to this city! On our first visit, we went up to the 4th floor for cocktails. I had a Pumpkin Spice Espresso Martini and my wife had a Matcha Margarita. Both drinks were outstanding! The next couple of mornings we stopped in for coffee, matcha, and pastries. The pastries here are not like the ones in regular Starbucks stores, they're amazing! My raspberry croissant was one of the best I've ever had!

The world's largest Starbucks! So a coffee lover like me surely has to pay homage! First tip- go early! The second floor pastry lounge opens at 8am and people line up early to get a good seat. It may need a little patience dealing with all these but if you can, the food here is amazing. The croissants had the crispiest crust I've ever tasted!!! Tip: if secondary floor is too crowded, try going up to the 3rd or 4th floors - we found some great seats up there.

Visited the Starbucks Reserve in Chicago and ordered an affogato along with a cold tiramisu latte. The affogato was a nice mix of smooth espresso over creamy gelato. The tiramisu latte had a dessert-like sweetness with a coffee backbone. The space itself is impressive, 4 floors, open design, and a lot of energy from the crowd. Overall, it's more of an experience than just grabbing coffee.

I drove from Cleveland Ohio just to see this roastery. I was impressed with it. They have four or five floors. Each floor had almost its own identity. I didn't know what floor to visit. A staff member was able to direct us. And I'm glad that she did. Because I was able to enjoy each floor in a reasonable amount of time.

Is it a tourist trap? Yes. Did it trap us? Also, yes. The Starbucks Reserve Roastery on Michigan Ave is a great example of just how much you can dress up a coffee bean. With 5 stories of bars. Unfortunately for us the 5th floor patio was closed but all other floors were open. The trick here is to go to at least the 3rd floor in order to get your drink due to the massive crowds on the first two floors. The 4th floor is a cocktail area. After reaching the 3rd floor bar we ordered their brownie, a pumpkin spice whiskey barrel-aged cold brew, and tiramisu latte. The drinks were insanely good.

10/1/25 Wednesday at 7:30 AM. The largest Starbucks in the world! It wasn't too crowded when I arrived but people started to trickle in shortly after. Floors 2 to 5 don't open till 8 AM so I ordered a drink while waiting. I got the dark chocolate mocha. Grande was the default size if you don't specify. The drinks here are marked up, $1-2 higher than normal Starbucks. Comparable to airport prices. Unfortunately I didn't get to see the roof level as it was closed for the season.

This was a cool spot to check out, especially as a tourist in Chicago! The line is long but it moved fast! Inside, the staff is super friendly and there's clear signage on what each level is and where to explore. We got a few coffees and pastries to share which were delicious (especially loved the olive oil cake we tried!) but my favorite had to be the espresso martini flight. I also loved seeing the classes and in-person experiences they offered. I wish there was more seating but we were able to finally find a quiet spot upstairs on the top floor.

The world's largest Starbucks Reserve Roastery is very impressive! It is 5 levels of Starbucks themed experience. L1 was the Starbucks Reserve Coffee Bar to go and all the Starbucks goods and merch. L2 - Starbucks Reserve Cafe where you can order specialty reserve coffee as well as food and desserts. L3 - Experimental Coffee Bar. Here you can drink speciality coffee flights and try the gelatos. L4 - Arriviamo Cocktail Bar. Coffee Espresso Martini Flights and other alcoholic drinks. L5 - Rooftop Terrace. We had the espresso martini flight and all the drinks were delicious. I bought the whiskey barrel coffee which is only available at this Chicago location.

My husband and I started off my birthday at the Starbucks Reserve Roastery. We went around 8 a.m.--there was a line, but it wasn't too bad. While my husband stood in line, I took advantage of the time and went upstairs to the bar area, where I ordered an Espresso Martini Pumpkin Spice Latte. By the time I came back down, he was almost ready to order. The food was just okay; I wasn't too impressed. However, the drinks were absolutely delicious--not just the espresso martini, but the Tiramisu Latte as well.

I had one goal while driving up to see family for Thanksgiving and that was to stop at the Starbucks Reserve in Chicago. I did just that! I went straight to the 4th floor and got the holiday espresso martini flight, which came with an eggnog martini, peppermint mocha martini and their classic espresso martini. The peppermint mocha one was probably my favorite. The flight was such a fun little treat. I also got the salami pizza for a snack. Before I left, I had to get the Chicago Reserve Starbucks cup and 2025 Christmas ornament. I left a happy person!

This starbucks was so beautiful to visit! The bar upstairs is soo beautiful. we got the holiday espresso martini flight and the drinks were delicious, I only didn't like the eggnog one. The service could've been a little better, we sat down at the bar expecting someone to greet us and got up after 5 min realizing we're supposed to order at the front and then sit. I wish that someone would've told us.

My friend and I almost skipped this Reserve after seeing the snake of a line, but curiosity and caffeine cravings won. With around 20-40 people ahead, we waited for about 15-20 minutes. Atmosphere: A massive coffee wonderland - think Costco, but for coffee nerds. Four floors in total - merch on the ground floor, food in the middle, a bar up top, and a bean-roasting contraption. Food: I chose a matcha butterfly swirl cake. My friend's sandwich fared better. Drinks: The free sample of Bourbon whiskey coffee was bold and interesting. I ordered the iced ube coconut latte. My friend's sakura float though? Absolutely delicious - 10/10 would steal sips again. Price: Higher than your neighborhood Starbucks, but that's the tradeoff for the novelty.

Every Starbucks fan loves Starbucks Reserve Roastery, and I am no different. I try to sample any new drinks that they come up with, and this time I had the Iced Ube Coconut Latte and I really loved it. There is usually a looong line for the second floor cafe and so I picked up the latte from the ground floor before heading for the food on the second floor. Since there were limited food option, I picked the savoury cornetto with prosciutto and cheese. It was alright - nothing extraordinary.

Just checked out this location after visiting the Reserve Roastery in New York. The staff are helpful and very informative and offer directions to various floors and products when you walk in. They also know a lot about coffee, roasting, the perfect grind for your needs, etc. They have fresh samples to try - I was able to sample a mini iced pumpkin spice coffee and a hot Chicago blend. Interestingly, there are escalators leading up to the second floor but stairs to get back down (or an elevator) on the opposite side of the floor.

Luckily we snagged a table for a party that didn't show up, but highly recommend making a reservation ahead of time. We ordered all 3 seasonal cocktails and the matcha butterfly swirl cake. Chef kiss to the lavender matcha margarita and the ube espresso martini. Those two tied for possibly one of the best cocktails I've ever had. The butterfly tea spritz was okay. Also ordered the ube coconut latte, but they had ran out of coconut milk so it was just an ube latte. The blueberry lavender cake donut wasn't too bad either. There's not much to their rooftop - fairly small.

Starbucks Reserve Roastery in Chicago has some snazzy offerings, some of which are unique and some that fall flat. Located on the Magnificent Mile, this location can get insanely busy. There are four stories of inside offerings and a fifth story of a teeny rooftop patio. The first story offers specialty coffees for purchase, as well as lots of Chicago Reserve-specific merchandise. The second and third floors offer a variety of coffees, exclusive Reserve drinks, and desserts as well as food. Fourth floor is mainly for cocktails and experiences. Chocolate hazelnut cornetto: Essentially a croissant with some piped in chocolate hazelnut spread. Chocolate brownie tart: Pretty decadent. Affogato: Hard to go wrong with ice cream and coffee. Pumpkin spice latte with real pumpkin puree: better than the traditional PSL syrup. Tiramisu latte: I didn't think this particularly reminded me of tiramisu, but it was tasty. Dark chocolate mocha: Tasted like a traditional mocha.

I have never been a Starbucks fan however my past two positive and enjoyable experiences at the Reserve Roastery might have converted me. For my first visit, our barista cousin was visiting from Hong Kong and we booked a Coffee Roasting and Tasting Experience for $75 per person on a Wednesday. For my second visit, I brought my parents, two young daughters, and the Mister for coffee and lunch on a Friday. We arrived before 10:30 am and there was a line out the door. We were in line for approximately 20 minutes and we received our food and drinks order within 10 minutes. The six of us had: Spirit-free whiskey cold brew with vanilla syrup, Toffeenut bianco latte, Crispy hashbrown egg cup, Fig walnut ricotta toast, Prosciutto and parmigiano-reggiano on herb cornetto, Salame milano on focaccia, Salame piccante, Pistachio and rose twice-baked cornetto, Chocolate cornetto, Plain cornetto, Chocolate ganache brownie.

OMG. Loved loved loved the Starbucks. We were a party of 12 six adults and five kids. The kids had a blast, ordering all kinds of pastries and trying different drinks. The adults went to the fourth floor and had the best espresso martinis and Matcha margaritas that you could imagine. It was fun to go to each floor and see what they had and offered. The staff was so super friendly and knowledgeable.

Get here as early as you can! We got here at 9AM and it was packed like a vacuum sealed bag of coffee beans. By the time we left they had people waiting outside wrapped around the building. First floor had exclusive merchandise and small food offerings and drinks. Second floor is the cafe with ALL the food and drinks available. Third floor had coffee flights and brewing experience. Fourth floor had the cocktail bar with signature and classic cocktails. Fifth floor had the rooftop terrace and Michigan avenue views with mostly reserved seats for the paid experiences. After ordering your food and paying with the Starbucks app, they give you a table buzzer thing. Try the seasonal items. I loved the lavender donut and rose pistachio croissant the best! Exclusive merchandise: Umbrella with Chicago and sax $40, Chicago roasted beans $28, Mugs and espresso cup with Chicago, Candyland Truck toy with 646 address $25. Drinks: Toffee Bianco Latte, Iced Ube Latte. Food: Lavender donut, Matcha butterfly swirl cake, Rose pistachio cornetto, Crispy hash brown egg cup, Prosciutto and parmigiano on herb cornetto.

This place is always packed, often with a line out front. We went in the late afternoon and were able to walk in, but the first floor was closed due to capacity, so we were directed to the second floor. We decided to check out the fourth floor for cocktails. The line was long, and seating was tight, but we got lucky and snagged a table after ordering. The seating situation can be a little confusing--some tables are marked as reserved and appear to have table service, while others are first come, first served.

The Starbucks Reserve Roastery is kind of like a theme park for coffee lovers. With 4 floors (plus the rooftop!) it's a huge space. Basically the first two floors offer the food and drinks. I think the first floor is mainly for drinks and pastries while the second floor offers a more extensive food menu. The 3rd floor is the Experiential Coffee Bar so you can see different ways they prepare coffee. The 4th floor is a cocktail bar that serves coffee and tea-themed drinks. And the rooftop is pretty small compared to the rest of the space. It took a while to get our order but the place was really, really busy. The prices are kind of steep, but isn't Starbucks known to be pricey?

I finally paid a visit to the Starbucks Reserve Roastery on mag mile a visit and wow, is this place cool! I've steered clear for many years of the long line around the door that lasted years after they first opened. Now that we are almost 6 years in, the buzz died down enough and I felt like it was time. Now that I've visited...I get it! Such a cool spot centered around a spiral escalator which leads to 5 different floors. The first floor specializes in Starbucks Reserve products, the second is bakery/cafe items, the third is experiential creations, fourth is a coffee cocktail bar, and the fifth floor is the rooftop. I landed on the experiential floor and had a lovely time. All the drinks are speciality ones you can only find here! I tried the iced ube coconut latte, Starbucks first ube beverage! Espresso and coconutmilk are poured over ice, topped with a ube coconut cold foam and sprinkled with coconut flakes. I liked that it wasn't overly sweet. I was able to snag a seat by the window and worked here for a couple hours. It was a great time! I definitely want to come back and experience the other floors.

Ordered: Classic Cornetto (2/5): This was basically an overpriced croissant that didn't taste super fresh. Was a bit disappointed because we were hoping to try another pastry but they had sold out on the floor we were on and we didn't want to move from our seats so settled for this. Cold brew trio flight (3.5/5): We preferred the whiskey, nitro, then lastly cold brew. This happens to also be the least to most acidic, so probably not a coincidence! Overall (4/5): Love the vibes and exploring but can get very busy. The menu looked very exciting so maybe we just got the wrong things but would be open to going back and trying something else since we enjoyed the interior so much.

I went to the Starbucks reserve on seattle and wasn't impressed. This one was 3 levels and rooftop area when it's not cold out so it definitely seemed more substantial than the brand's hometown. The coffee beans for their special beans are pretty expensive but i assume thats because you get them freshly bagged and they can grind them for you too. What's cool is here you can actually do a tour and see the process. They also have a variety of sandwiches, pastries and even boozy beverages. I was actually able to get a tea that I really enjoyed that's not available in the regular stores. It's a neat place to visit and they have more options than a regular Starbucks, some items are pricier but it was just as busy as a typical Starbucks

This is my 2nd reserve after the Seattle one. This building is huge, has 4 stories to accommodate all the tourists. I ordered some pastry on the 2nd floor then went up to the 4th floor for the bar, ordered their famous Whiskey Cloud. It arrived in 3 containers for self assembly, coffee, liquors, and cream. It was a big portion and tasted fabulous alongside the pastries, looking out the big windows next to my best friends. What a great experience.

I'm providing a rating for the fourth floor (cocktail bar) only. We went on a Friday at about 2pm. Waited about 10 min in the line to get into the reserve which went by super fast. Overall the place was super busy and it took about 10 min before the bartenders were able to get our orders in. We ordered the espresso martini flight. It was good but not entirely impressive since the only distinguishing difference between the martinis were the syrups they used to make different flavors. The flavors were very much okay, with the orange being too sweet, the mole having a nice strong kick of spice, and the cherry limone being my favorite with a good amount of sweetness (not too sweet). I wouldn't personally drink the flight again and order something else.

What a fun place! I've been here with friends who are visiting. It's such a busy but popular place and worth all the wait. The espresso martini flight is fab. They change up espresso martinis for the season. Their pizzas are surprisingly extremely tasty. The crust is a perfect flaky buttery. You really won't get bad food here. Just have patience here. The lines go by fast. It's hard to find a table that's the only down side. It's also a fun place to just walk through and see all the floors.

This is a 5 story Starbucks with each floor holding different menu and theme. We sat in the 4th floor of the restaurant where the cocktail bar is located. You can walk up the bar and order drinks and find a place to enjoy it or ask for the reserved seating with the hostess if available. We tried a few signature drinks like the martini flight, cold brew with whiskey and the whiskey cloud. The cold brew from the Guatemala region coffee (only available at this location) was amazingly smooth and strong. Overall good vibes, a bit of a hyped place but worth a try when visiting Chicago downtown. Be prepared for the long queue outside but it moves pretty quickly.

This is the largest Starbucks Reserve in the world. It is 4 stories of lux coffee experience with a drink menu, pastry offering, and modern decor space worthy of the Reserves tag. It's comfortable and a bit pretentious. I love visiting Starbucks Reserves in Tokyo and this takes me back to Tokyo time and time again. On today's adventure I tried the ube and coconut latte. My fear about flavored lattes is that they are too sweet but this latte is very balanced. I love the coconut chunks in the drink which gives it a nice texture balance. We also tried the coffee flight and it was interesting to hear everyone's favorite coffee and why. The coffee flight is large. We made the mistake of ordering two. One order can easily be shared amongst 3 caffeine addicts. Good service. Please come and create your own experience.

ORDER Ube Coconut Latte - The ube cream was really yummy. Not a huge fan of the coconut flakes since I'm not a coconut girly, but the latte was alright. The combination was a little bit funny, but the coffee was strong and good. Matcha Butterfly Swirl Cake - not super sweet which I prefer. Matcha flavor is really good and the butterfly pea cream isn't as flavored like butterfly pea tea but it's still good. VIBES This is one of 6 locations in the world. This location is 4 floors. The first floor is grab and go coffee with coffee beans sold. The second floor is the bakery. The third floor is specialty coffees and the fourth floor is the bar and alcoholic drinks. There's lots of seating and the view is beautiful. SERVICE Everyone was super nice and helpful!! Service is phenomenal and welcoming.

Not nearly enough seating in this 4 story Reserve. Every single time I go to this cafe I am lucky to even get a spot at the bar. Although I come here for studying- there is only one outlet in the ENTIRE building. One. Single. Outlet. This is a cafe... used for studying, they get a lot of college students, you would think that they would have outlets at every table. This confuses me, and leads me to end my time at the cafe. A lot of times I can't even order my food. I am guarding my spot the entire time which makes it so I can't leave my spot because it will be taken instantly. It's absurd the amount of people to table ratio it's so stupid. Not worth the drinks either you can get them at any local coffee shop or bar.

Gets very crowded, even on weekdays! Food is okay and everything is very expensive, although you are paying for the atmosphere. Hard to get any seating both times I've been there and ended up standing. Espresso Martini Flight 6/10 so strong in a not-so-yummy way and the flavors were okay Matcha Lavendar Margarita 10/10 barely tastes like a drink, the foam is so good. The pastries were okay but not worth the price

there was a line to the corner when we went, but it moved pretty fast. however, it was deceiving since the lines inside were even longer! went immediately up to the top so we could order cocktails, since the reserves are the only place that have alcohol. got the seasonal pumpkin spice espresso martini flight, and tried to order a pastry but they were all out of the one my friend wanted. seating is hard to find so hopefully there are multiple people in your party so while 1 person waits in line the other person can find a table. we took our drinks upstairs and enjoyed the fresh air and sipped on our martinis. they were pretty good. def not a place to relax like a normal coffee shop since all the lines are long and the tables are hard to come by. def a get 1 thing, finish it, and leave. tried to go back early the next day when they opened. but apparently they dont actually fully open when they say they open. when they open the only thing thats actually open is the first floor grab and go coffee and pastry and merch area. the upstairs doesnt open until later. first time being in a reserve so it was a good experience.

While visiting home for the holidays, we stopped by the Starbucks Reserve around 11am on a Monday afternoon and I was completely blown away with how beautiful this Starbucks was. Once we arrived, the line was wrapped around the building but thankfully, the line moved fast and before we knew it, we were inside. I was very excited to try the food and their specialty drinks but boy was it packed. It took us quite some time to even find a table. My sister and I had to divide and conquer because the cafe (which served the food) was on the 2nd floor but the coffee bar was on the 3rd floor so I stood in one line while my sister stood in another and both lines were very long. We ordered a few different drinks and food items but unfortunately, the food took over 30 mins to be ready. The croissant sandwiches were decent but our favorite was the Eggs in Purgatory. I did venture to the 4th floor because I had to try an espresso martini! I decided on the Eggnog Espresso Martini and it was worth every penny! Funny enough, that was the only floor that was quiet and not packed with people. Once we were done, we headed to the rooftop on the 5th floor and the views were amazing! With this being my first time visiting the Starbucks Reserve, I may give it one more shot and visit again some time in the future. It was a nice experience but I wasn't a fan of the crowds, long lines and mediocre food.
